question: What was in the beginning with God (Jn 1:1)?

options: Repent, for the kingdom is near

question: How were all things made (Jn 1:3)?
What was the life that was in the Word (Jn 1:4)?
What has not overcome the light (Jn 1:5)?

options: From a city on a hill

question: Why did John come (Jn 1:7)?
options: To call the people to repentance
As a witness to the light

question: Why did John testify concerning the light (Jn 1:7)?
options: So that the light would not be extinguished
So that all people might have life
So that all people might believe
So that all may know the light

question: To what does the true light give light (Jn 1:9)?
options: To those who follow the light into the world
To everyone coming into the world
To those who open their eyes to see the light

question: To what did he come (Jn 1:11)?
options: Those who would believe in him

question: What was the Word’s relationship to his own (Jn 1:11)?
options: They did not listen to him

question: What did the Word give to those believing in his name (Jn 1:12)?
options: The ability to follow the lamb wherever he goes
The right to become children of God

question: What did the Word become (Jn 1:14)?
Whose glory have we seen (Jn 1:14)?

As the law came through Moses, what came through Jesus Christ (Jn 1:17)?
Who has no one ever seen (Jn 1:18)?

question: What did John say about who he was, using the words of Isaiah (Jn 1:23)?
options: The one preparing the way before the Son of David
The voice of one calling in the wilderness
Elijah who is to come in the days of the Messiah

question: Who asked John why he baptized if he were not the Messiah (Jn 1:24)?
With what did John say he baptized (Jn 1:26)?

The one who the prophets spoke about

question: What did John say he was not worthy to do for the one coming after him (Jn 1:27)?
options: Tie a belt around his waist
Untie the straps of his sandals

question: What had the one who sent John told him how to identify the One coming (Jn 1:33)?
options: The man who would seek to be baptized by you in the Jordan River
The man on whom you see the Spirit come down and remain on

question: What did the one who sent John say the one coming would do (Jn 1:33)?
options: Come with healing in his wings
Suffer at the hands of men

How can he be from Galilee of the Gentiles?
Can any good thing come out of Nazareth?

question: What did Jesus first say of Nathanael as he approached Jesus (Jn 1:47)?
options: Here is a true Israelite, in whom there is nothing false
Here is a man who fears God and shuns evil

options: The Spirit descending like a dove
A bush burning that doesn’t burn up

Because he spoke with authority and not as the Pharisees

Those born from above and below

By obeying the commands of the Father

options: He has not heeded the word of God
He has rejected the Holy One of God

options: Because they did not go to the light

question: Why do people love darkness more than light (Jn 3:20)?
options: So they can do evil all they want
So that they will not be blinded by the light

options: So that all may come to believe in the one and only Son of God
So that he may testify of the truth

question: Who has eternal life (Jn 3:36)?

He will be cast out of the kingdom of God

question: What will happen to the one who rejects the Son (Jn 3:36)?
options: He will be thrown out into outer darkness

options: That he was a friend of tax-collectors and sinners

They had gone to the city gate to get a donkey

options: Because it was improper for men to make such requests of women
Because she was drawing water only for her family

question: Jesus told the Samaritan woman she should have asked for what from him (Jn 4:10)?

options: Are you greater than our father Abraham who drank at this well

options: A spring of water to eternal life

question: When asked about her husband, what did the woman of Samaria say (Jn 4:17)?
options: He was on a long trip

options: He went into hiding at the spring of En Gedi

options: Poured expensive perfume on Jesus’ feet

options: Brought in the news about Jerusalem
Wiped Jesus perfumed feet with her hair

options: He cared deeply for the poor, the fatherless, and widows
He wanted the perfume so he could use it to gain favors from others
